query:
SELECT 
  id, 
  name, 
  slug,
  atoll,
  overview,
  bestTimeToVisit,
  currency,
  language,
  latitude,
  longitude,
  topThingsToDo,
  quickFacts,
  attractions,
  beachesLocalRules,
  foodCafes,
  faq,
  LENGTH(overview) as overview_length,
  LENGTH(topThingsToDo) as activities_length
FROM island_guides 
ORDER BY name
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 3FFv3TtDUeNbYDM.root --database JUCCYykxYWxAcNny3wop55 --execute SELECT 
  id, 
  name, 
  slug,
  atoll,
  overview,
  bestTimeToVisit,
  currency,
  language,
  latitude,
  longitude,
  topThingsToDo,
  quickFacts,
  attractions,
  beachesLocalRules,
  foodCafes,
  faq,
  LENGTH(overview) as overview_length,
  LENGTH(topThingsToDo) as activities_length
FROM island_guides 
ORDER BY name

rows.name: Alifushi
rows.overview: Alifushi is a charming local island nestled in the Raa Atoll of the Maldives, renowned primarily for its traditional boat building industry. Unlike the more commercialized resort islands, Alifushi offers an authentic glimpse into Maldivian island life and culture. It's a peaceful escape, characterized by pristine white-sand beaches, crystal-clear turquoise waters, and a laid-back atmosphere. Visitors can explore the local community, witness skilled craftsmen at work, and immerse themselves in the genuine hospitality of the islanders. Ideal for travelers seeking a cultural experience combined with the natural beauty of the Maldives, Alifushi promises a unique and memorable adventure away from the typical tourist crowds.
rows.topThingsToDo: [{"emoji":"🚤","title":"Explore the Boat Building Yards","description":"Witness the traditional craft of Maldivian 'dhoni' construction, observing skilled artisans at work. Alifushi is famous for this industry."},{"emoji":"🐠","title":"Snorkeling and Diving","description":"Discover the vibrant underwater world surrounding Alifushi, teeming with colorful coral reefs and diverse marine life. Local guides can arrange trips."},{"emoji":"🏖️","title":"Relax on Local Beaches","description":"Enjoy the serene and uncrowded white-sand beaches, perfect for sunbathing, swimming, and watching breathtaking sunsets."},{"emoji":"🚶","title":"Wander Through the Village","description":"Stroll through the local streets, interact with friendly islanders, and experience the authentic pace of Maldivian island life."},{"emoji":"🎣","title":"Sunset Fishing Trip","description":"Join a traditional Maldivian fishing excursion as the sun sets, a popular activity offering a taste of local customs and fresh catch."}]
rows.quickFacts: ["Located in Raa Atoll, Maldives.","Known as the 'Boat Building Island' of the Maldives.","Offers an authentic Maldivian cultural experience.","Accessible by domestic flight to Ifuru Airport (IFU) followed by a speedboat transfer.","Primarily a local island, guesthouse accommodation is available.","Alcohol is not permitted on local islands.","Dress modestly when in public areas out of respect for local culture.","The official language is Dhivehi, but English is understood in guesthouses."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Alifushi Boat Building Yards","description":"The heart of Alifushi's industry, where visitors can observe the intricate process of building traditional Maldivian 'dhonis' from scratch."},{"name":"Alifushi Beach","description":"The main stretch of pristine white sand, offering tranquil spots for relaxation and swimming in the clear lagoon."},{"name":"Local Mosque","description":"A glimpse into the island's religious and cultural life, showcasing traditional Maldivian architecture."},{"name":"Local Schools and Community Centers","description":"While not tourist attractions in the traditional sense, these offer insight into daily life and community structure on a local island."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Local Cafes & Tea Shops","description":"Experience local Maldivian snacks (hedhikaa) and traditional tea at small, informal cafes catering primarily to residents. Ask locals for recommendations."},{"name":"Guesthouses with Dining","description":"Some guesthouses on the island offer dining services for their guests, often featuring local Maldivian cuisine and fresh seafood."}]

rows.name: Bodufolhudhoo
rows.overview: Bodufolhudhoo is a charming local island nestled in the Alif Alif Atoll of the Maldives, offering an authentic Maldivian experience away from the bustling resort islands. Known for its pristine white sandy beaches, crystal-clear turquoise waters, and vibrant house reef, it's a paradise for snorkelers and divers. The island maintains a peaceful, laid-back atmosphere, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in local culture and witness daily island life. With a focus on sustainable tourism, Bodufolhudhoo provides an idyllic escape for those seeking natural beauty, marine adventures, and genuine Maldivian hospitality.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Snorkeling", "Diving", "Island Hopping", "Local Cuisine", "Photography"]
rows.quickFacts: ["Located in Alif Alif Atoll (North Ari Atoll), Maldives.","A local inhabited island, offering an authentic Maldivian experience.","Known for its pristine bikini beach and vibrant house reef.","Accessible by public ferry, speedboat, or seaplane from Malé.","Population is relatively small, fostering a peaceful atmosphere.","Alcohol is not permitted on local islands due to Maldivian law.","Dress modestly when outside the bikini beach area.","Excellent destination for snorkeling, diving, and marine excursions."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Bodufolhudhoo Bikini Beach","description":"The island's main tourist beach, offering soft white sand, calm turquoise waters perfect for swimming, and sunbathing facilities."},{"name":"House Reef","description":"The vibrant coral reef encircling the island, easily accessible from the beach, providing excellent snorkeling opportunities with diverse marine life."},{"name":"Local Mosque","description":"A modest yet significant landmark reflecting the island's cultural and religious heritage."},{"name":"Local Fishing Harbor","description":"Witness local fishermen bringing in their daily catch and experience a glimpse of the island's primary industry."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Island Cafe","description":"A local eatery offering traditional Maldivian dishes, fresh seafood, and international options in a casual setting."},{"name":"Local Guesthouses' Restaurants","description":"Many guesthouses on the island offer their own dining experiences, serving delicious home-cooked meals, often featuring fresh catches of the day."}]

rows.name: Dhigurah
rows.overview: Dhigurah, meaning 'Long Island' in Dhivehi, is a picturesque local island in the South Ari Atoll of the Maldives, renowned for its stunningly long, pristine white sand beaches and a sandbank stretching into the turquoise ocean. Unlike many resort islands, Dhigurah offers an authentic Maldivian experience, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in local culture while enjoying world-class natural beauty. It's particularly famous as one of the best places globally to swim with whale sharks year-round, making it a diver's and snorkeler's paradise. The island balances tranquil relaxation with exciting marine adventures, all within a welcoming community setting.
rows.topThingsToDo: [{"emoji":"🦈","title":"Swim with Whale Sharks","description":"Dhigurah is a prime location for encountering majestic whale sharks, often seen year-round in the surrounding waters of South Ari Atoll. Join a guided tour for an unforgettable snorkeling or diving experience."},{"emoji":"🏖️","title":"Relax on the Sandbank","description":"Walk along the island's famous long sandbank, which extends far into the ocean, offering breathtaking views and a perfect spot for sunbathing, photography, and enjoying the serene environment."},{"emoji":"🐠","title":"Snorkeling & Diving","description":"Explore vibrant coral reefs teeming with diverse marine life, including manta rays, turtles, and colorful fish. Several dive centers offer courses and excursions for all skill levels."},{"emoji":"🌅","title":"Sunset Cruise","description":"Embark on a traditional Maldivian Dhoni boat for a magical sunset cruise, often accompanied by playful dolphins, offering spectacular views as the sun dips below the horizon."},{"emoji":"🚶","title":"Explore Local Life","description":"Wander through the island's village, observe daily life, interact with friendly locals, and experience the authentic culture of the Maldives away from resort settings."}]
rows.quickFacts: ["Located in South Ari Atoll, Maldives.","Famous for year-round whale shark encounters.","One of the longest islands in the Maldives.","Features a stunning, extensive sandbank.","Local island, offering an authentic Maldivian experience.","Guesthouse accommodation is prevalent.","Alcohol is not permitted on local islands.","Accessible by domestic flight or speedboat from Malé."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Dhigurah Beach & Sandbank","description":"The island's main draw, featuring miles of soft white sand and a stunning sandbank that provides picturesque views and ample space for relaxation."},{"name":"Whale Shark Point","description":"The designated area in the South Ari Atoll where whale shark encounters are most frequent, a must-visit for marine enthusiasts."},{"name":"Local Village","description":"Experience authentic Maldivian culture by strolling through the village, observing local life, and visiting the mosque and local shops."},{"name":"Coral Gardens","description":"Numerous vibrant coral reefs surrounding the island, easily accessible for snorkeling and diving, showcasing the rich biodiversity of the Maldivian waters."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Hermit's","description":"A popular spot offering a mix of Maldivian and international dishes, known for its fresh seafood and beachfront dining experience."},{"name":"Dhigurah Retreat Cafe","description":"Located within a guesthouse, this cafe serves delicious local and Western breakfast, lunch, and dinner options in a cozy setting."},{"name":"Maldives Whale Shark Research Programme (MWSRP) Cafe","description":"A small cafe associated with the research program, often offering light bites, refreshments, and a chance to learn about conservation efforts."}]

rows.name: Feidhoo
rows.overview: Feidhoo, often spelled Fuvahmulah, is a unique and captivating island in the Maldives, distinguished by its single-island atoll status. Unlike most Maldivian islands, it is not a coral atoll but a large, isolated landmass with a rich freshwater lake system and diverse topography, including wetlands, lush vegetation, and even small hills. This geological anomaly makes Feidhoo a biodiversity hotspot, offering a different Maldivian experience away from the typical resort islands. It's known for its vibrant local culture, friendly communities, and a slower pace of life, providing an authentic glimpse into Maldivian traditions. The island is also gaining recognition as a prime destination for diving enthusiasts, particularly for its encounters with tiger sharks and thresher sharks.
rows.topThingsToDo: [{"emoji":"🦈","title":"Tiger Shark Diving","description":"Fuvahmulah is world-renowned for its consistent encounters with tiger sharks. Dive centers offer guided dives to specific sites where these magnificent creatures are frequently spotted, providing an exhilarating and unforgettable experience for certified divers."},{"emoji":"🎣","title":"Local Fishing Trip","description":"Join local fishermen for a traditional Maldivian fishing experience. Try your hand at hand-line fishing for snapper, grouper, or even tuna, and enjoy the tranquility of the open ocean as the sun sets."},{"emoji":"🛶","title":"Explore the Freshwater Lakes","description":"Discover the island's unique freshwater lakes, Bandaara Kilhi and Dhadimagi Kilhi. Rent a kayak or paddleboat to explore these serene wetlands, home to diverse birdlife and lush vegetation, offering a peaceful escape."},{"emoji":"🚶‍♀️","title":"Island Cycling & Nature Walks","description":"Rent a bicycle or take a leisurely walk to explore the island's diverse landscapes. Cycle through palm groves, past taro fields, and along the coast, discovering hidden spots and enjoying the natural beauty."},{"emoji":"🏖️","title":"Relax at Thoondu Beach","description":"Visit Thoondu Beach, famous for its unique round pebbles and strong currents that create a natural 'washing machine' effect. It's a picturesque spot for sunbathing, enjoying the unique geology, and watching local life."},{"emoji":"🍲","title":"Savor Local Cuisine","description":"Indulge in authentic Maldivian dishes at local guesthouses or small eateries. Don't miss out on fresh seafood, traditional curries, and local snacks like 'hedhikaa', experiencing the true flavors of the island."}]
rows.quickFacts: ["Feidhoo is also known as Fuvahmulah, meaning 'Island of Areca Nuts'.","It is the only single-island atoll in the Maldives, making it geologically unique.","The island boasts two freshwater lakes, a rarity in the coral-dominated Maldives.","Fuvahmulah is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve candidate due to its unique biodiversity.","It's considered one of the best places in the world for tiger shark and thresher shark diving.","The local language is Dhivehi, but English is commonly understood in tourist areas.","The island has a domestic airport (Fuvahmulah Airport - FVM) connecting it to Malé.","Alcohol is prohibited on local islands in the Maldives, including Fuvahmulah."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Bandaara Kilhi","description":"One of the two large freshwater lakes on the island, surrounded by lush vegetation, offering a tranquil setting for nature lovers and birdwatchers."},{"name":"Dhadimagi Kilhi","description":"The second significant freshwater lake, known for its scenic beauty and as an important ecological site, ideal for kayaking and exploring the unique ecosystem."},{"name":"Thoondu Beach","description":"A distinctive beach famous for its smooth, round pebbles and the unique 'Thoondu' phenomenon where strong currents create a natural washing machine effect, especially during certain seasons."},{"name":"Fuvahmulah Lighthouse","description":"A modern lighthouse offering panoramic views of the island and surrounding ocean, a great spot for sunset photography."},{"name":"Old Friday Mosque (Bandaara Miskiy)","description":"A historic mosque showcasing traditional Maldivian architecture and craftsmanship, offering a glimpse into the island's rich cultural heritage."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Fuvahmulah Inn Restaurant","description":"A popular spot offering a mix of Maldivian and international cuisine, known for its fresh seafood and friendly atmosphere."},{"name":"The Chill Spot Cafe","description":"A cozy cafe perfect for a light meal, coffee, or fresh juices, often frequented by both locals and tourists."},{"name":"Island Bites","description":"A local eatery serving traditional Maldivian short eats (hedhikaa), curries, and rice dishes, providing an authentic taste of the island."}]

rows.name: Fulidhoo
rows.overview: Fulidhoo is a charming local island nestled in the Vaavu Atoll of the Maldives, renowned for its pristine natural beauty and tranquil atmosphere. Unlike the bustling resort islands, Fulidhoo offers an authentic Maldivian experience, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in local culture and enjoy unspoiled landscapes. The island is characterized by its soft white sand beaches, crystal-clear turquoise lagoons, and vibrant coral reefs teeming with marine life. It's a haven for snorkelers and divers, promising encounters with sharks, rays, and colorful fish. Life on Fulidhoo moves at a slower pace, inviting relaxation and a genuine connection with nature. It's an ideal destination for travelers seeking a peaceful escape, adventurous water activities, and a glimpse into traditional Maldivian island life, all while enjoying the warm hospitality of its small community.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Snorkeling", "Diving", "Local Culture", "Beach Relaxation", "Water Sports"]
rows.quickFacts: ["Located in Vaavu Atoll, Maldives.","A local island, offering an authentic Maldivian experience.","Known for excellent snorkeling and diving opportunities.","Home to a designated 'bikini beach' for tourists.","Accessible by public ferry, speedboat, or private transfer from Malé.","Alcohol is not permitted on local islands.","The island is small enough to explore entirely on foot.","Supports a small, friendly community with guesthouses and local cafes."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Fulidhoo Bikini Beach","description":"The main tourist beach on the island, offering soft white sand, clear waters, and a designated area for tourists to wear swimwear. It's ideal for swimming, sunbathing, and enjoying the lagoon views."},{"name":"Fulidhoo Coral Reefs","description":"The extensive coral gardens surrounding the island are a major draw, easily accessible for snorkeling and home to diverse marine ecosystems. Key spots include the house reef and nearby channels."},{"name":"Local Village & Harbor","description":"The heart of Fulidhoo, where you can observe daily life, see traditional Maldivian houses, and watch local fishermen bring in their daily catch at the small harbor."},{"name":"Vaavu Atoll Channels & Thilas","description":"While not directly on Fulidhoo, these world-renowned dive sites (like Fotteyo Kandu, Miyaru Kandu) are easily accessible via boat excursions from the island, offering incredible drift dives and encounters with large pelagics."}]

rows.name: Hithadhoo
rows.overview: Hithadhoo is the second-largest island in the Maldives and the most populous island in Addu Atoll, located in the southernmost part of the archipelago. Unlike typical resort islands, Hithadhoo offers an authentic glimpse into local Maldivian life, culture, and community. It's connected by causeways to several other islands within Addu Atoll, including Maradhoo, Feydhoo, and Gan, making exploration easy. The island is known for its lush greenery, historical sites, and vibrant local atmosphere. Visitors can experience traditional Maldivian hospitality, explore local markets, and enjoy the natural beauty of its beaches and marine life, all while being immersed in a non-touristy environment.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Diving", "Snorkeling", "Surfing", "Fishing", "Beach Activities"]
rows.quickFacts: ["Hithadhoo is the second-largest island in the Maldives by land area.","It is the most populous island in Addu Atoll.","Hithadhoo is connected to several other islands by causeways, forming a continuous landmass.","The island offers an authentic local Maldivian experience, distinct from resort islands.","Addu Atoll was a British Royal Air Force base (RAF Gan) until 1976.","The local language is Dhivehi, with a unique Addu dialect.","The currency used is the Maldivian Rufiyaa (MVR).","Hithadhoo is home to Addu National Park, known for its wetlands and birdlife."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Addu National Park (Koattey)","description":"A protected area on the northern tip of Hithadhoo, featuring diverse ecosystems including mangroves, wetlands, and a rich birdlife, offering nature trails and viewpoints."},{"name":"Hithadhoo Old Friday Mosque","description":"An ancient mosque showcasing traditional Maldivian coral stone architecture and intricate carvings, offering a glimpse into the island's historical and religious heritage."},{"name":"Hithadhoo Local Market","description":"A bustling market where locals buy and sell fresh produce, fish, and local goods, providing an authentic cultural experience."},{"name":"Addu Scuba Club & Dive Center","description":"While not an 'attraction' in the traditional sense, it's a key point for accessing the stunning underwater attractions of Addu Atoll, offering diving and snorkeling excursions to various reefs and shipwrecks."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Palm Village Restaurant","description":"A popular local eatery offering a mix of Maldivian and international cuisine, known for its fresh seafood and relaxed ambiance."},{"name":"Cafe' Royal","description":"A local favorite for quick bites, coffee, and traditional Maldivian snacks (hedhikaa), perfect for experiencing local flavors."},{"name":"Addu City Cafe'","description":"Offers a range of Maldivian dishes, juices, and some Western options in a casual setting, popular with both locals and visitors."}]

rows.name: Hulhumalé
rows.overview: Hulhumalé is a reclaimed island in the Maldives, strategically located just north of Malé and connected to Velana International Airport by a causeway. Developed to alleviate congestion in the capital and provide more land for housing and tourism, it offers a fascinating blend of local Maldivian life and modern infrastructure. Unlike the private resort islands, Hulhumalé provides an authentic glimpse into the local culture, with guesthouses, local eateries, and public beaches. It serves as an excellent base for budget-conscious travelers or those with layovers, offering easy access to the airport and ferry connections to Malé, while still providing beautiful beaches and water activities. Its planned layout, green spaces, and relaxed atmosphere make it a comfortable and convenient gateway to the Maldivian experience.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Snorkeling", "Diving", "Beach Relaxation", "Water Sports", "Local Exploration"]
rows.quickFacts: ["Hulhumalé is a reclaimed island, purpose-built to expand living space and tourism in the Maldives.","It is directly connected to Velana International Airport (MLE) by a causeway, making it highly accessible.","The island offers a more local and authentic Maldivian experience compared to private resort islands.","Public transport includes buses connecting to the airport and ferries to Malé.","Alcohol is generally not permitted on Hulhumalé due to local regulations, unlike private resorts.","Dress code on public beaches and areas requires modest attire (shoulders and knees covered).","Accommodation options primarily consist of guesthouses and budget-friendly hotels.","It serves as an excellent base for exploring the Malé Atoll and accessing various water activities."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Hulhumalé Central Park","description":"A green oasis in the heart of the island, offering walking paths, benches, and a playground, ideal for relaxation and recreation."},{"name":"Hulhumalé Beach","description":"The main public beach on the island, featuring white sand and clear waters, suitable for swimming and sunbathing, with designated areas for local dress codes."},{"name":"Hulhumalé Ferry Terminal","description":"While primarily a transport hub, it's a bustling area offering views of local life and boats, connecting Hulhumalé to Malé and the airport."},{"name":"Hulhumalé Phase 2","description":"An ongoing expansion project showcasing the future development of the island, with new residential and commercial areas taking shape."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"The Keyolhu","description":"A popular spot known for its diverse menu, including Maldivian, Indian, and continental dishes, with a pleasant ambiance."},{"name":"Shell Beans","description":"A well-known coffee shop chain in the Maldives, offering a variety of coffees, pastries, sandwiches, and light meals in a cozy setting."},{"name":"Bombay Darbar","description":"Offers authentic Indian cuisine, popular for its curries, tandoori dishes, and vegetarian options, providing a flavorful dining experience."},{"name":"O' View Restaurant","description":"Located with a view, this restaurant serves a mix of local and international dishes, often praised for its fresh seafood and relaxed atmosphere."},{"name":"Family Room Cafe","description":"A local favorite for its affordable and delicious Maldivian short eats (hedhikaa), juices, and light meals, perfect for a quick bite."}]

rows.name: Inguraidhoo
rows.overview: Inguraidhoo is a serene local island nestled in the Raa Atoll of the Maldives, offering an authentic glimpse into Maldivian island life away from the bustling tourist resorts. Known for its pristine natural beauty, the island boasts soft white sandy beaches, crystal-clear turquoise waters, and vibrant coral reefs teeming with marine life. It's a relatively small, inhabited island, providing a peaceful escape for travelers seeking cultural immersion and unspoiled nature. Visitors can experience the warmth of local hospitality, observe traditional fishing practices, and unwind in a tranquil environment. The island is less developed for tourism compared to resort islands, making it an ideal destination for those who appreciate simplicity, local charm, and a genuine connection with the Maldivian way of life.
rows.topThingsToDo: [{"emoji":"🐠","title":"Snorkeling and Diving","description":"Explore the vibrant house reefs surrounding Inguraidhoo, teeming with colorful fish, sea turtles, and various coral species. Local dive centers or guesthouses can arrange excursions to nearby dive sites."},{"emoji":"🎣","title":"Sunset Fishing Trip","description":"Join local fishermen for a traditional Maldivian sunset fishing experience. Learn hand-line fishing techniques and enjoy the serene beauty of the Maldivian sunset over the ocean."},{"emoji":"🏖️","title":"Relax at the Bikini Beach","description":"Unwind on the designated 'Bikini Beach' where tourists can comfortably sunbathe and swim. Enjoy the soft sand and the calm, warm waters."},{"emoji":"🚶","title":"Island Walk & Local Culture","description":"Stroll through the island's sandy paths, observe daily local life, visit the mosque, and interact with friendly residents to get a true sense of Maldivian culture and hospitality."},{"emoji":"🐬","title":"Dolphin Watching Excursion","description":"Embark on a boat trip to spot playful pods of dolphins in their natural habitat, often seen leaping and swimming alongside the boat."}]

rows.name: Kadhdhoo
rows.overview: Kadhdhoo is a historic domestic airport island constructed with international support (1982-1986), serving as the gateway to Laamu Atoll. This unique island combines historical significance with modern airport facilities. Perfect for travelers seeking unique island experiences with historical context.

rows.name: Kamadhoo
rows.overview: A home to Kamadhoo Inn, Kamadhoo is a charming local island in Baa Atoll, Maldives. With white, sandy beaches, turquoise lagoons, and excellent diving opportunities, it offers an authentic island experience.

rows.name: Kandooma
rows.overview: Kandooma is a scenic island with beautiful beaches and excellent diving spots.
rows.topThingsToDo: [{"emoji":"🤿","title":"House Reef Snorkeling","description":"Discover the vibrant marine life and coral formations just steps from the shore around Kandooma island's extensive house reef."},{"emoji":"🏄","title":"Kandooma Thila Diving","description":"Experience world-class diving at Kandooma Thila, a renowned dive site famous for its strong currents, grey reef sharks, eagle rays, and abundant pelagic fish."},{"emoji":"🌅","title":"Sunset Dolphin Cruise","description":"Embark on a traditional Maldivian Dhoni boat to witness playful dolphins leaping through the waves as the sun sets over the Indian Ocean."},{"emoji":"🎯","title":"Surfing at Kandooma Right","description":"Catch perfect waves at Kandooma Right, a private surf break accessible directly from the island, suitable for intermediate to advanced surfers."},{"emoji":"👥","title":"Island Hopping & Local Village Visit","description":"Explore nearby local islands to experience authentic Maldivian culture, interact with residents, and learn about their traditional way of life."},{"emoji":"💧","title":"Overwater Spa Treatment","description":"Indulge in a rejuvenating massage or wellness treatment at the overwater spa, offering serene ocean views and ultimate relaxation."},{"emoji":"🐠","title":"Night Fishing Experience","description":"Try your hand at traditional Maldivian night fishing, using hand lines to catch reef fish under the stars, followed by a BBQ of your catch."},{"emoji":"🏖️","title":"Beachfront Dining Experience","description":"Enjoy a romantic dinner under the stars with your feet in the sand, featuring fresh seafood and international cuisine, accompanied by the sound of the waves."}]

rows.overview: Kudahuvadhoo features a nature park with coastal scrub, mangroves, and stunning reef views. Perfect for nature lovers seeking pristine environments and genuine cultural interactions away from commercial tourism.

rows.name: Maalhos
rows.overview: Maalhos is one of the inhabited islands of Baa Atoll in the Maldives. Home to Madi Finolhu Guest House, Maalhos is located in the only UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and it's just a couple of minutes away from Hanifaru Bay.

rows.name: Maamigili
rows.overview: Maamigili is a local island in Alifu Dhaalu Atoll, home to Villa International Airport. The island offers authentic Maldivian experiences with local communities, traditional fishing practices, and access to pristine diving sites. It serves as a gateway to the central-south atolls.
rows.topThingsToDo: [{"emoji":"🤿","title":"Whale Shark Snorkeling Excursion","description":"Embark on a boat trip to the South Ari Atoll Marine Protected Area (SAMPA), a renowned spot for swimming alongside majestic whale sharks, the gentle giants of the ocean."},{"emoji":"👥","title":"Maamigili Local Island Tour","description":"Wander through the local streets of Maamigili, observe daily life, interact with residents, and experience the authentic culture of a Maldivian fishing community."},{"emoji":"🏄","title":"PADI Certified Diving at Local Reefs","description":"Explore the vibrant underwater world of Alifu Dhaalu Atoll with certified dives to pristine reefs, encountering diverse marine life including colorful corals, reef sharks, and turtles."},{"emoji":"🐠","title":"Sunset Fishing Trip","description":"Join local fishermen on a traditional Dhoni boat as the sun sets, learning Maldivian hand-line fishing techniques and trying your luck at catching dinner."},{"emoji":"🎯","title":"Kayaking or Stand-Up Paddleboarding","description":"Rent a kayak or stand-up paddleboard and explore the calm, turquoise lagoons surrounding Maamigili at your own pace, enjoying the serenity of the ocean."},{"emoji":"🏖️","title":"Beach Relaxation and Stargazing","description":"Spend a peaceful evening on the island's secluded beaches, enjoying the tranquility and gazing at the brilliant, unpolluted night sky filled with stars."},{"emoji":"🎯","title":"Visit Villa International Airport (VAM)","description":"Observe the operations of a local airport, watch seaplanes take off and land, and experience the unique atmosphere of a small island aviation hub."}]

rows.name: Madifushi
rows.overview: Madifushi Private Island is a luxurious and serene escape nestled in the pristine Meemu Atoll of the Maldives. This exquisite resort offers an unparalleled blend of natural beauty, sophisticated comfort, and authentic Maldivian hospitality. Surrounded by crystal-clear turquoise lagoons, vibrant coral reefs, and powdery white-sand beaches, Madifushi is a haven for those seeking relaxation, romance, and adventure. Guests can indulge in world-class dining, rejuvenating spa treatments, and a myriad of water sports, all while experiencing the breathtaking tranquility of this secluded paradise. The island prides itself on its commitment to sustainability and providing an unforgettable, personalized experience for every visitor.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Diving", "Snorkeling", "Surfing", "Fishing", "Beach Activities"]
rows.quickFacts: ["Located in Meemu Atoll, Maldives.","Accessible by seaplane transfer from Malé International Airport (Velana International Airport - MLE).","Known for its luxurious overwater and beach villas.","Offers a range of water sports and diving excursions.","Features multiple dining options with international and local cuisine.","Home to a pristine house reef for snorkeling.","Emphasizes sustainable tourism practices.","Ideal for honeymoons, romantic getaways, and family vacations."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"House Reef","description":"The vibrant coral reef encircling the island, easily accessible for snorkeling and home to a rich diversity of marine life."},{"name":"Madifushi Beach","description":"Pristine white-sand beaches stretching around the island, ideal for sunbathing, leisurely strolls, and relaxation."},{"name":"Overwater Villas","description":"The iconic Maldivian accommodation, offering direct lagoon access and breathtaking ocean views, a highlight of the island's charm."},{"name":"Infinity Pool","description":"The resort's stunning main pool, seemingly blending into the ocean, offering a refreshing dip and panoramic views."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Bluefin","description":"The resort's main restaurant offering an international buffet with live cooking stations and themed nights."},{"name":"The Wharf","description":"An à la carte specialty restaurant serving fresh seafood and grilled dishes in an elegant overwater setting."},{"name":"Thundi","description":"A casual beachfront bar and restaurant perfect for light bites, refreshing cocktails, and stunning ocean views."},{"name":"Sunset Pool Bar","description":"Located by the main pool, offering a selection of drinks, snacks, and light meals with a perfect view of the sunset."}]

rows.name: Malé
rows.overview: Malé is the heart of the Maldives, serving as the political, economic, and cultural center. The city is home to the stunning Grand Friday Mosque, colorful local markets, and numerous restaurants and shops. Despite being densely populated, Malé offers authentic Maldivian culture and is an excellent starting point for exploring the islands.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Snorkeling", "Diving", "Beach Relaxation", "Water Sports", "Local Exploration"]

rows.name: Maradhoo
rows.overview: Maradhoo is one of the inhabited islands of Addu Atoll, the southernmost atoll of the Maldives. Known for its tranquil atmosphere and lush greenery, Maradhoo offers a glimpse into authentic Maldivian island life away from the typical resort hustle. The island is connected to several other islands within Addu Atoll by a causeway, making exploration of the wider atoll convenient. Visitors can experience local culture, enjoy pristine beaches, and witness the daily rhythms of a close-knit community. Its calm lagoons and vibrant marine life also make it an appealing destination for water activities and relaxation.
rows.topThingsToDo: [{"emoji":"🚴","title":"Cycle the Addu Link Road","description":"Rent a bicycle and explore the scenic causeway connecting Maradhoo to other islands like Hithadhoo and Feydhoo, enjoying coastal views and local life."},{"emoji":"🐠","title":"Snorkeling and Diving","description":"Discover the vibrant coral reefs and diverse marine life in the clear waters surrounding Maradhoo. Local dive centers can arrange trips to popular spots."},{"emoji":"🏖️","title":"Relax on Local Beaches","description":"Unwind on the tranquil, uncrowded beaches of Maradhoo, enjoying the sun, sand, and the gentle lapping of the Indian Ocean."},{"emoji":"🎣","title":"Sunset Fishing Trip","description":"Join a traditional Maldivian fishing trip during sunset, a popular local pastime, and try your hand at catching dinner."},{"emoji":"🚶","title":"Explore Local Villages","description":"Wander through the streets of Maradhoo and neighboring islands, observing local architecture, engaging with residents, and experiencing authentic Maldivian culture."}]
rows.quickFacts: ["Maradhoo is part of Addu Atoll, the southernmost atoll of the Maldives.","It is an inhabited island, offering an authentic local experience.","Connected by causeway to other islands like Hithadhoo, Feydhoo, and Gan.","The primary language spoken is Dhivehi, with some English understood in tourist-facing areas.","The local currency is Maldivian Rufiyaa (MVR), but USD is widely accepted.","Alcohol is not available on inhabited islands due to local laws, except in resort hotels.","Dress modestly when walking around the island, especially outside guesthouses.","The island is accessible via domestic flight to Gan International Airport (GAN), followed by a short transfer."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Addu Link Road","description":"A significant causeway connecting several islands of Addu Atoll, offering picturesque views and a great way to explore the region by bike or foot."},{"name":"Maradhoo Harbor Area","description":"The bustling heart of the island, where local boats (dhonis) come and go, offering a glimpse into the daily life and fishing industry."},{"name":"Local Mosques","description":"Observe the simple yet elegant architecture of the island's mosques, which serve as community focal points."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Local Tea Shops (Hotels)","description":"Experience authentic Maldivian snacks (hedhikaa) and local short eats, along with traditional tea or coffee, at small, community-run 'hotels'."},{"name":"Maradhoo Cafe/Restaurant (various small establishments)","description":"Dine at one of the modest local eateries offering Maldivian cuisine, including fresh seafood, curries, and rice dishes, at affordable prices."}]

rows.name: Muli
rows.overview: Muli is the capital island of Meemu Atoll in the Maldives, offering a glimpse into authentic Maldivian island life away from the typical resort experience. Known for its laid-back atmosphere and stunning natural beauty, Muli is a popular destination for surfers, divers, and those seeking a peaceful escape. The island boasts pristine white-sand beaches, crystal-clear turquoise waters, and vibrant coral reefs teeming with marine life. While not as developed for tourism as some other islands, Muli provides essential services and a friendly local community. Visitors can immerse themselves in local culture, enjoy water sports, or simply relax and soak in the serene environment. It's an ideal spot for travelers looking for an adventurous yet tranquil Maldivian experience.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Snorkeling", "Diving", "Local Culture", "Beach Activities", "Photography"]
rows.quickFacts: ["Muli is the capital island of Meemu Atoll, Maldives.","It is a popular destination for surfers, known for the 'Farms' surf break.","The island offers an authentic Maldivian local island experience, away from resorts.","Surfing season typically runs from April to October.","Accommodation primarily consists of local guesthouses.","The primary language spoken is Dhivehi.","Alcohol is not permitted on local islands in the Maldives.","The island is accessible via domestic flight to Maafaru (NMF) or speedboat from Malé."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Muli Harbor","description":"The bustling heart of the island, where local dhonis (traditional boats) come and go. It's a great place to observe daily island life, watch fishermen unload their catch, and witness the comings and goings of local transport."},{"name":"Muli Mosque","description":"A prominent landmark on the island, showcasing traditional Maldivian Islamic architecture. While generally not open for non-Muslim visitors, its exterior offers a glimpse into the island's cultural and religious heritage."},{"name":"Local Streets and Houses","description":"Wandering through the residential areas provides an authentic cultural experience. Observe the unique architecture, vibrant colors, and the peaceful, communal lifestyle of Muli's inhabitants."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Muli Cafe (Local Teashop)","description":"A simple, local teashop offering traditional Maldivian short eats (hedhikaa), fresh juices, and hot beverages. It's a great spot to mingle with locals and experience authentic Maldivian snacks."},{"name":"Local Guesthouses with Restaurants","description":"Many guesthouses on Muli offer in-house dining for their guests, often serving a mix of Maldivian and international cuisine. These meals often feature fresh seafood caught locally."}]

rows.name: Mundoo
rows.overview: Mundoo is a secluded local island in Laamu Atoll featuring pristine beaches and excellent marine life. This authentic island offers genuine Maldivian culture and access to pristine natural beauty. Perfect for travelers seeking secluded island experiences with authentic cultural immersion.

rows.name: Thulusdhoo
rows.overview: Thulusdhoo is a vibrant local island in Ari Atoll known for its excellent water sports, friendly community, and authentic Maldivian culture. The island is famous for its world-class surf breaks, particularly Chickens and Pasta Point, making it a paradise for surfers. With beautiful beaches, local restaurants, and a laid-back atmosphere, Thulusdhoo offers an authentic island experience.

rows.name: Veymandoo
rows.overview: Veymandoo is the capital island of Thaa Atoll (Kolhumadulu Atoll) in the Maldives. Unlike many resort islands, Veymandoo offers a glimpse into authentic Maldivian island life, away from the typical tourist crowds. It's a relatively small, inhabited island known for its local charm, friendly community, and untouched natural beauty. Visitors can experience the slow pace of island living, interact with locals, and enjoy the pristine surroundings. While not a major tourist hub, it provides a unique opportunity for those seeking cultural immersion and a tranquil escape, with basic amenities and guesthouses available for a genuine Maldivian experience.
rows.topThingsToDo: ["Diving", "Snorkeling", "Surfing", "Fishing", "Beach Activities"]
rows.quickFacts: ["Veymandoo is the capital island of Thaa Atoll (Kolhumadulu Atoll).","It is an inhabited island, offering a glimpse into local Maldivian life.","The primary language spoken is Dhivehi.","Tourism infrastructure is minimal, focusing on guesthouses rather than resorts.","Alcohol is not permitted on local islands like Veymandoo.","The island is accessible by domestic flight to Thimarafushi Airport (TMF) followed by a speedboat transfer, or by a longer public ferry.","The local economy relies heavily on fishing and government administration.","Dress modestly when walking around the island as a sign of respect for local culture."]
rows.attractions: [{"name":"Veymandoo Harbor","description":"The main hub of activity, where local boats (dhonis) come and go. It's a great spot to observe local life and the island's connection to the sea."},{"name":"Local Mosque","description":"An important religious and architectural landmark on the island, showcasing traditional Maldivian Islamic design."},{"name":"Island Streets and Houses","description":"The charm of Veymandoo lies in its simple, well-kept streets and colorful local houses, offering a genuine insight into Maldivian island living."},{"name":"Local Beaches","description":"While not designated 'attractions' in the typical sense, the pristine and quiet local beaches are a major draw for relaxation and swimming."}]
rows.foodCafes: [{"name":"Local Cafes & Restaurants","description":"Veymandoo has a few small, local cafes and restaurants that offer traditional Maldivian meals, short eats (hedhikaa), and basic beverages. These are typically family-run establishments providing an authentic taste of the island."},{"name":"Guesthouse Dining","description":"Many guesthouses on Veymandoo offer meal plans for their guests, providing home-cooked Maldivian and international dishes, often prepared with fresh local ingredients."}]

180018	Feidhoo	feidhoo	Seenu Atoll	Feidhoo, often spelled Fuvahmulah, is a unique and captivating island in the Maldives, distinguished by its single-island atoll status. Unlike most Maldivian islands, it is not a coral atoll but a large, isolated landmass with a rich freshwater lake system and diverse topography, including wetlands, lush vegetation, and even small hills. This geological anomaly makes Feidhoo a biodiversity hotspot, offering a different Maldivian experience away from the typical resort islands. It's known for its vibrant local culture, friendly communities, and a slower pace of life, providing an authentic glimpse into Maldivian traditions. The island is also gaining recognition as a prime destination for diving enthusiasts, particularly for its encounters with tiger sharks and thresher sharks. 180021	Veymandoo	veymandoo	Thaa Atoll	Veymandoo is the capital island of Thaa Atoll (Kolhumadulu Atoll) in the Maldives. Unlike many resort islands, Veymandoo offers a glimpse into authentic Maldivian island life, away from the typical tourist crowds. It's a relatively small, inhabited island known for its local charm, friendly community, and untouched natural beauty. Visitors can experience the slow pace of island living, interact with locals, and enjoy the pristine surroundings. While not a major tourist hub, it provides a unique opportunity for those seeking cultural immersion and a tranquil escape, with basic amenities and guesthouses available for a genuine Maldivian experience.
